_meta.source: National Park Service, state summary pages (unit counts, FY2020 vintage); NPS Land Resources Division quarterly Acreage Report, 'Acreage by State' (acreage, as of 2026-06-30)
_meta.notes: Count of National Park Service-managed units per state (all designation types: national parks, monuments, historic sites, seashores, parkways, recreation areas, etc.), not limited to units carrying the literal 'National Park' designation; every state's nps.gov/state/{code} page carries an identical footnote stating unit-count figures are for the fiscal year ended 2020-09-30, so treat those as approximate/dated pending verification. Acreage figures are from NPS's own official 'Acreage by State' aggregation (not a third-party re-derivation), current as of 2026-06-30; multi-state units (e.g. Yellowstone spanning ID-MT-WY, Blue Ridge Parkway spanning NC-VA) are pre-attributed to each state by NPS internally — the public per-unit listing does not expose that split, so NPS's own state totals were used directly rather than approximated. Not comparable in vintage to the unit counts. This is a proxy for density/scale of federally-managed outdoor recreation access, distinct from env.public_land_pct, which measures overall public land ownership share (BLM, state, tribal, etc.) regardless of recreational development.
